commands.author: ubehera

target.title: fix(agents): gate owned-write publish on pre-append fingerprint (#86572)
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a pre-append trust gate at the publish-time write boundary so the embedded session takeover fence cannot misclassify pi's own writes as external mutation. Complementary to [#87159](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/87159), which serializes cross-attempt access to the same session file but does NOT cover same-lane writes between `releaseForPrompt` and the next hook lock acquisition. Verified to still hold post-#87159 + #87409 rebase.

Closes #86572. Defense-in-depth alongside #87159.

## Rebase note (force-pushed 2026-05-29, head `d780c41bf1`)

Rebased onto current `upstream/main`, picking up:
- [#87159](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/87159) — canonical session-file ownership indexing
- [#87409](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/87409) — removed `installSessionExternalHookWriteLock` and `installSessionEventWriteLock`, moved hook locking into the controller

Conflicts resolved with the architectural-invariant lens:
- Dropped the install-* wrapper machinery from the patch (obsolete after #87409)
- Kept the publish gate (`publishOwnedPostMessageWrite(beforeWrite)`, `beforeMessagePersist` opt) — still load-bearing
- Dropped 12 install-* dependent tests; will add focused unit tests for the publish gate in a follow-up

Net diff post-rebase: +142 / -8 across 5 files.

## Why this is still needed

@htuyer121 [reported in a comment](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/86584#issuecomment-4562247920) (and in [#86845](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/86845)) that the `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` class is **still firing in production post-#87159**: 30 events on disk across 11 jobs and 4 agents, all `--session isolated` (single-lane), rate accelerating, spread widening to voice-TTS paths.

`--session isolated` is the key signal: #87159 prevents two attempts from entering the same session file's prompt window. It does NOT prevent a single attempt's own pi append (via `_handleAgentEvent → sessionManager.appendMessage → _persist → appendFileSync`) between `releaseForPrompt` and the next hook lock acquisition from being misclassified as external mutation. That's what this PR's pre-append trust gate addresses.

The two fixes are orthogonal:
- **#87159**: serializes attempts at the file-ownership boundary (cross-lane invariant)
- **#86584**: distinguishes the lane's own writes from external mutation at publish time (same-lane invariant)

Both invariants are needed to close the takeover-error class observed in production.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** Eliminates `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` on the lane's own pi-driven writes when an external hook fires after rapid pi writes. The takeover fence still trips on genuine external mutations AND on mixed external-then-pi interleaves.

- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw source checkout (macOS 26.5, Node 26) at the rebased branch HEAD `d780c41bf1`, branched off `468b971fba` (current `upstream/main`, post-#87159 + #87409). The proof harness loads the rebased controller from source and drives four scenarios end-to-end against real temp session files via `fs.appendFile` and `controller.withSessionWriteLock`.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

  ```sh
  pnpm install
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.test.ts
  npx tsx /Users/umank/code/openclaw-tickets/proof/run-refresh-before-hook-lock-proof.mjs
  ```

- **Evidence after fix (post-rebase proof harness):**

  ```
  ============================================================
  PR #86584 — publish-on-onMessagePersisted proof (Issue #86572)
  ============================================================
  source under test: src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.ts
                     publishOwnedPostMessageWrite(beforeWrite)
                     gated on isTrustedSessionFileState(key, beforeWrite)

  -------- Scenario A — pre-fix: pi-style write WITHOUT publish (trips takeover) --------
    pi-write    pi appendFileSync (F0 → F1) — no publish call
    hook        controller.withSessionWriteLock(beforeToolCallBody) — fence check happens here
    hook        RESULT: TAKEOVER (EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError)
    state       hasSessionTakeover() = true

  -------- Scenario B — post-fix: pi-style write WITH publish (hook fires cleanly) --------
    pi-write    pi appendFileSync (F0 → F1) with pre-write captured
    publish     publishOwnedPostMessageWrite(F0) — F0 is trusted, record F1 as owned
    hook        controller.withSessionWriteLock(beforeToolCallBody) — fence check happens here
    hook        RESULT: hook fired successfully (hookOutcome.fired=true)
    state       hasSessionTakeover() = false

  -------- Scenario C — negative: external write bypasses publish (still trips) --------
    pi-write    EXTERNAL appendFileSync (F0 → F1) — no publish
    hook        RESULT: TAKEOVER (EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError)

  -------- Scenario D — mixed interleave: external THEN pi write + publish (still trips) --------
    external    EXTERNAL appendFileSync first (F0 → F1)
    pi-write    pi appendFileSync second (F1 → F2) — pre-write captured = F1
    publish     publishOwnedPostMessageWrite(F1) — F1 NOT trusted, skip (fail closed)
    hook        RESULT: TAKEOVER (EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError)

  RESULT: PASS — all four scenarios behaved as expected.
  ```

  Scenario D is the bot-required negative regression (mixed external-then-pi interleave). It still trips on the rebased code — proving the trust gate's invariant survives the cross-cutting changes from #87159 and #87409.

- **Test suite:** existing 80 cases under `embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.test.ts` pass cleanly post-rebase. The 12 publish-related tests our PR added pre-rebase depended on `installSessionExternalHookWriteLock` (removed by #87409); they'll be re-added as focused unit tests against `controller.withSessionWriteLock(...)` in a follow-up commit. Marking this PR as the publish gate's core fix; the test re-add is a separate concern.

- **Observed result after fix:** The publish gate behaves identically on the new architecture. Same-lane pi writes get correctly recorded as owned when the pre-append fingerprint is trusted; external writes (including those interleaved before pi's append) still trip the fence.

- **What was not tested:** A live reproduction against htuyer121's 30-event repro data. They [offered the .jsonl files](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/86584#issuecomment-4562247920); validating against that would be the strongest possible signal but I don't have the corpus locally yet.

## Compatibility / Risk

- `publishOwnedPostMessageWrite(beforeWrite)` is an additive method on `EmbeddedAttemptSessionLockController`. Existing call sites unaffected.
- `beforeMessagePersist` is an additive optional opt on `session-tool-result-guard`. Backward compatible.
- The trust gate is fail-closed: any baseline mismatch refuses to record. Cannot launder external mutations.

## Related

- Issue #86572 — root cause analysis and acceptance criteria
- #85913 — original tracking issue (closed by #87159)
- #86845 — htuyer121's production corroboration
- #87159 — joshavant's session-file ownership (complementary, landed)
- #87409 — moved hook locking into controller (this PR rebased on top)

---

*This PR is AI-assisted. Code authored with Claude.*
target.author: ubehera
